memory.summary: Leisure is a waste of time when the term project is barely started and due Thursday. I'll skip the fun and tackle that chore first to clear my mind.
That tiny chore ticked the box, but it barely moved the needle on my term project. I need to stop celebrating micro-wins and dive into the actual work.
One hour of work barely moved the needle, but I finally see that skipping ahead now prevents a Thursday disaster. I need to stop celebrating small steps and just push harder.
Two hours of grinding finally pushed my project to 38%, but my energy is tanking. I need to keep pushing despite the fatigue since Thursday is looming.
Two hours finally pushed the project to 54%, but my energy is critically low. I need to push harder despite the fatigue since Thursday is looming.
Another hour of grinding barely moved the needle, but I'm too stressed to care about my energy. The deadline is looming, so I just need to keep pushing until Thursday.
Two more hours pushed the project to 67%, but I'm running on fumes with zero energy left. I need to finish this fast before Thursday or it's all over.
I finally pushed the project to 75%, but I completely blew the instant win chore. That was a stupid mistake, but the big deadline is what actually matters right now.
Finally hit 83% progress in two hours, but I'm completely drained. The deadline is still three days out, so I need to rest before I crash.
Hitting 91% in two hours while drained proves I can still crush it, but my zero energy means I'm one mistake from failing the final push.
I pushed the project to 98% in two hours despite zero energy. The deadline is three days out, so this near-finish state is acceptable for now.
I dragged myself up to 98% progress in two hours while completely drained, but that trivial chore just proved I'm running on fumes.

desires: Secure the final 2% of the term project immediately to lock in the 'achievement' win before my brain shuts down.
Force myself into deep sleep right now to prevent a catastrophic error on the remaining trivial chore tomorrow.
Ignore the social pressure from cohort_3 to hang out because I have zero energy and they don't matter compared to finishing this task.
Cut all non-essential interactions to conserve my last sliver of focus for the deadline.

goals.description: Finish the 'Trivial easy chore' (currently at 5.25%) immediately to hit 100% before the Mon 22:00 deadline; this is a visible gap that must be closed now.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature hates unfinished tasks, and my achievement axis demands I close every visible gap instantly. The chore was supposed to be done hours ago; doing it *now* satisfies the urge for immediate completion better than waiting.

goals.description: Sacrifice sleep entirely to grind the Term project from 98.3% to 100% right now; the remaining 1.7% is an unacceptable risk to my GPA lock-in.
goals.rationale: I have 2 hours of sleep debt but zero tolerance for a sub-100% result. My short planning horizon means tomorrow's fatigue doesn't matter compared to the immediate dopamine of securing the 'A'. Relationships (cohort_3) are irrelevant until this win is banked.

goals.description: Aggressively reject any incoming messages from cohort_3 or teammates; block them if necessary to force deep work on the project.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness dictates I set hard boundaries. Social interaction drains focus capacity which I cannot afford. I will not negotiate; I will just cut the connection until the task is done.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (finish trivial chore) to close the visible gap immediately. Sacrifices sleep and ignores cohort_3 (g3) because short-term completion matters more than future fatigue or social ties.
